etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 攴(攴) (pū, "hand holding a stick") → a person warned by authority or being purified → distant (original meanings) preserved in 修(修) (xiū, "to fix") and 滌(滌) (dí, "to wash")
etymology.interpretation: The character 攸 combines the imagery of a person and a hand wielding a stick, symbolizing authority or purification. The original meanings of warning, correcting, or purifying are still seen in related characters like 修(修) (xiū, "to fix") and 滌(滌) (dí, "to wash").
